items.headline: Mixed Signals as S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit Record Highs, Dow Dips
items.summary: The U.S. stock market closed out the week with a mixed performance on Friday, May 2, 2026. Both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite reached new record highs, propelled by robust earnings from major technology companies. However, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a slight decline, indicating a divergence in market leadership. Overall sentiment remained cautious amid ongoing concerns about inflation, elevated oil prices due to Middle East tensions, and a perceived 'froth' in some market segments. AI-linked investments continued to drive significant movements, though investors showed increased scrutiny of capital expenditure plans.

items.headline: Big Tech Earnings Propel Markets; AI Capex Under Scrutiny
items.summary: Major technology companies continued to influence market movements this week. Alphabet ($GOOGL) exceeded expectations, with shares rising approximately 10% on Thursday, April 30, following strong earnings. Apple ($AAPL) also contributed to market strength, jumping 3.2% on Friday after reporting higher-than-expected fiscal second-quarter profits. Conversely, Meta Platforms ($META) disappointed investors with increased capital expenditure forecasts for AI and a decline in monthly active users, causing its stock to fall. Microsoft ($MSFT) also saw its shares decline despite beating earnings, as increased AI-related spending and expected drops in its PC and gaming segments raised concerns. The market is now closely scrutinizing AI capital expenditures, rewarding those tied to revenue acceleration and penalizing those without clear monetization paths.

items.headline: Inflationary Pressures Persist Amid Mixed Economic Data
items.summary: Economic data released this week painted a mixed picture, with persistent inflationary pressures. The Core PCE, a key inflation gauge, rose 0.3% month-over-month and 3.2% year-over-year, marking its highest core reading since November 2023. Initial jobless claims for the week ended April 25 hit a 50-year low of 189,000, suggesting a strong labor market. However, the April Michigan sentiment was revised down to a record low of 49.8, with year-ahead inflation expectations spiking to 4.7%. ISM Manufacturing held steady at 52.7, but the prices-paid component reached a four-year high. The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady midweek, with Chairman Powell maintaining a balanced tone.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Keep Oil Prices Elevated; S&P 500 Inclusion for Veeva Systems
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran, continued to keep oil prices elevated, with Brent crude pushing above $111 per barrel. This contributes to broader inflationary concerns and remains a key risk for the markets. In other news, Veeva Systems ($VEEV) saw its shares jump approximately 10-11% in after-hours and pre-market trading, following the announcement of its inclusion in the S&P 500 index, effective May 7, 2026.

items.headline: Berkshire Hathaway Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. ($BRK.A, $BRK.B) announced its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Saturday, May 2, 2026. The company reported net earnings attributable to shareholders of $10.106 billion, a significant increase from $4.603 billion in the first quarter of 2025. This strong performance was primarily driven by operating earnings of $11.346 billion.
